codeReview.summary: Feature community-templates successfully implemented GitHub issue and PR templates. Issue templates exist in .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/ (bug_report.yml, feature_request.yml) and PR template exists as .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md. All templates are properly formatted (YAML validated, Markdown valid). Templates guide contributors with spec vs tooling classification dropdowns/options.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The validation-contract.md evidence check for VAL-GH-003 uses '[ -d ".github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE" ]' (directory check) but GitHub's standard convention for PR templates is a single file '.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md', not a directory. The implementation correctly uses the file-based approach. The validation contract could be updated to reflect the correct GitHub convention.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md line ~490: 'Evidence: [ -d ".github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE" ] || [ -d ".github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE" ]' vs actual file at .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md

summary: Feature community-templates PASSES review. Created .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.yml (105 lines) with spec/tooling classification, .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/feature_request.yml (93 lines) with spec/tooling classification, and .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md (104 lines) with PR type classification (Spec/Tooling/Docs/CI/CD/Community). All templates properly guide contributors for spec vs tooling changes as required by VAL-GH-003.
